title: hymenophyllum macgillivrayi
intro: Hymenophyllum macgillivrayi, also known as Macgillivray's filmy fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to Australia, and is known for its delicate, lacy fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in infusions or decoctions, and has been traditionally used to treat colds, flu, and other infectious diseases.
